zh.lifeSummary: 當Sarah Elizabeth Martin於1385年4月7日出生在英國時，她的父親Martin31歲，母親Martin26歲。她和Theodore Benson Hardinger至少有2個女兒。她去世於1440年2月17日在她的家鄉享年54歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sarah Elizabeth Martin于1385年4月7日出生在英国时，她的父亲Martin31岁，母亲Martin26岁。她和Theodore Benson Hardinger至少有2个女儿。她去世于1440年2月17日在她的家乡享年54岁。

nameMeanings.name: Sarah
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Arabic/Muslim Farid, Abdel, Bachar, Jamal, Khaled, Laila, Munir, Said.
Americanized form of Italian Sara 1 and Hungarian Sára, influenced by the English form of the Biblical personal name Sarah.

English (Cornwall and Devon): variant of Sare with final -e spelled -a(h) (see Sara 2), a common feature of names in Devon and Cornwall. Spellings of the surname with -ah may have been influenced by the form of the personal name in the King James Bible.

variants.name: Sara
meanings: Italian and Jewish; Hungarian (Sára): from the female personal name Sara, borne in the Bible by the wife of Abraham, mother of Isaac. According to the Book of Genesis she was originally called Sarai (said to mean ‘contentious’ in Hebrew), but had her name changed by God to the more auspicious Sarah ‘princess’ in token of a greater blessing (Genesis 17:15, ‘And God said unto Abraham, As for Sarai thy wife, thou shalt not call her name Sarai, but Sarah shall her name be’). Compare Sarah 1.

English (Cornwall): from the Middle English and Old French female personal name Sare, Sarre (modern English Sarah 2), Latinized as Sarra, and borrowed from Greek Sarra, a Hellenized form of Hebrew Sarai (see 1 above). The personal name was widely used in medieval England, especially in the southern and eastern counties. Compare Sarratt and Sarson .
Spanish; unexplained. It is found in Navarre and Extremadura.

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Dominique.

English: from the female personal name Elizabeth, which owed its popularity in medieval Europe to the fact that it was borne by John the Baptist's mother. The original form of the name was Hebrew Elisheva ‘my God (is my) oath’ and it appears thus in Exodus 6:23 as the name of Aaron's wife. By New Testament times the second element had been altered to Hebrew shabat ‘rest, Sabbath’. The personal name became even more popular after the canonization of Elisabeth, Duchess of Thuringia around 1230. See also Isabel .

West Indian (Dutch Caribbean): from the female personal name Elizabeth (see 1 above). It is one of the surnames (compare Martina ) that reflect the partially mother-oriented name culture of the formerly enslaved people in the West Indies.

variants.name: Behlen
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Fritz, Kurt.
German: metronymic from Abele or Hebele, pet forms ofthe female personal name Elisabeth (see Elizabeth ), thesuffix -n being the (weak) genitive ending; thus ‘(husband orson) of Bele’.

variants.name: Beilke
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erwin, Kurt, Gerhard, Helmuth, Otto.
German:
from a pet form of Beile, itself a pet form of Elisabeth (see Elizabeth ).

variants.name: Isabel
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Alfonso, Alvarado, Augusto, Belia, Josefina, Juan, Manuel, Mario, Romero, Santa.

French, Spanish, Portuguese, and English (of French origin): from the Old French female personal name Isabel, which is first recorded in late 11th-century Paris and Maine (France). It was an alternative form of Elizabeth , Old French Elisabet, which had a shortened pet form Isabet. Isabel appears to have originated as a variant of Isabet, in which the suffix -el was substituted for -et through re-interpretation of -et as a hypocoristic diminutive. The resultant -bel was early associated with Old French bel ‘fair, beautiful’, as is evident in the French variant Isabeau. From France the name was taken to England, where it achieved considerable popularity in the late medieval period as an independent personal name alongside its rarer doublet Elizabeth. In the form Isabel it also appears in Spain and Portugal, while in Italy it took the form Isabella . The French surnames Isabel and Isabelle are particularly associated with Normandy.
History: Michel Isabel from Reux in Calvados, France, married Marie Jobidon in Château-Richer, QC, in 1673.

variants.name: Elsenpeter
meanings: German: variant of Elspeter, an extinct metronymic from a vernacular form of the personal name Elisabeth (see Elizabeth ) or a habitational name for someone from Elsbethen near Salzburg, Austria. The surname Elsenpeter is very rare in Germany.

variants.name: Elser
meanings: German: metronymic from the personal name Else, a short form of Elisabeth (see Elizabeth ).
North German: topographic name for someone who lived near alders, from Low German else ‘alder’.
North German: habitational name for someone from any of the places called Elsen in Rhineland and Westphalia.

variants.name: Delisa
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Dino, Gino, Romeo.
Italian (De Lisa or D'Elisa): metronymic, with the preposition d(e) ‘of’, from the female personal names Lisa or Elisa, short forms of Elisabetta, an equivalent of Elizabeth .

variants.name: Betten
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Claus, Dieter, Manfred.
Dutch and North German: metronymic from the personal name Bette, a shortened form of Elisabeth (see Elizabeth ), or a patronymic from Bet, a pet form of Berthold (see Beth ).

variants.name: Elsner
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Wolfgang, Alfons, Dieter, Egon, Ernst, Erwin, Eward, Hans, Heinz, Lutz, Manfred.
German:

northern topographic name for someone who lived near alders, from Low German else ‘alder’. It may also be a habitational name for someone from a place called with this word. In Silesia it has the same meaning, but is of ultimate Slavic origin. This surname is also found elsewhere in central Europe, e.g. in Czechia and Slovenia, where it may (also) be a translation into German of corresponding Slavic surnames.

variants.name: Elsen
meanings: German, Flemish, and Dutch: metronymic from a short form of the female personal name Elisabeth (see Elizabeth ).
German; Dutch (also Van Elsen): habitational name from a place so named in North Rhine-Westphalia.

Flemish and Dutch (also Van Elsen): habitational name for someone from any of various places called Elzen, for example in North Brabant in the Netherlands and in the provinces of Brabant and Liège in Belgium.

variants.name: Elsbernd
meanings: German: metronymic name meaning ‘Bernd, son of Els’, Els here a short form of the personal name Elisabeth (see Elizabeth ).

nameMeanings.name: Martin
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Scottish, Irish, French, Walloon, Breton, Dutch, Flemish, German, Czech, Slovak, Croatian, Italian (Veneto); Spanish (Martín): from a personal name derived from Latin Martinus, itself a derivative of Mars, genitive Martis, the Roman god of fertility and war, whose name may derive ultimately from a root mar ‘gleam’. This was borne by a famous 4th-century Christian saint, Martin of Tours, and consequently became extremely popular throughout Europe in the Middle Ages. In North America, the surname Martin has absorbed cognates and derivatives from other languages, e.g. Slovak and Rusyn (from Slovakia) Marcin , Albanian Martini , Polish surnames beginning with Marcin-, and Slovenian patronymics like Martinčič (see Martincic ). Martin is the most frequent surname in France and one of the most frequent surnames in Wallonia.
English: variant of   Marton  .
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Mártain, ‘descendant of Martin’ (compare 1 above). Otherwise, a shortened form of Gilmartin or McMartin ; sometimes also spelled Martyn.

variants.name: Marczewski
meanings: Polish: habitational name for someone from Marcewo (formerly also Marczewo) in Greater Poland Voivodeship. Alternatively, a derivative of the personal name Marc, Marcz, a pet form of Marcin, Marek (see Martin , Mark ).

variants.name: Marciniak
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Eugeniusz, Ewa, Agnieszka, Bartosz, Boleslaw, Ignatius, Jaroslaw, Jerzy, Krystyna, Ryszard, Slawomir, Stanislawa.
Polish: patronymic from the personal name Marcin (see Martin ).

variants.name: Marak
meanings: Czech:
(Marák): from a pet form of the personal names Martin or Marek .
(Mařák): metronymic from the female personal name Máří, Czech form of Maria . It may also denoted the husband of a woman named Máří.

variants.name: Machain
meanings: Basque: Castilianized form of Matxain, a topographic or habitational name referring to a house named with a diminutive of the personal name Martin .

variants.name: Maertens
meanings: Flemish and Dutch: patronymic from the personal name Maerten, a variant of Martin .
North German (Märtens): see Martens .

variants.name: Marcin
meanings: Slovak, Rusyn (from Slovakia), and Polish: from Marcin, a personal name equivalent to Martin . In Poland this is the most usual form as a personal name, but the surname is very rare there, while in Slovakia, where Marcin is a dialect form of the name Martin, the situation is opposite.
American shortened form of Polish Marcinkiewicz and other derivatives of the personal name Marcin (see 1 above).
In some cases possibly also French: from a vernacular form of the personal name Marcien, derived from the Latin surname Marcianus, popularized through the cult of a local saint.

variants.name: Macho
meanings: Spanish: nickname from macho ‘male; mule’ (from Latin masculus ‘male, masculine’), applied either to denote a virile, strong man or alternatively an idiot.
Spanish: possibly also a nickname for a forceful person or a metonymic occupational name for a smith, from macho ‘sledgehammer’ (from Latin marculus ‘hammer’).
Slovak: from a derivative of the personal name   Martin  .

variants.name: Marcinowski
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Aleksander, Bogdan, Jerzy, Zbigniew, Zigmund.
Polish: habitational name for someone from any of the places called Marcinów, Marcinowa, Marcinowice, or any of numerous other places in Poland named with the personal name Marcin (see Martin ).

variants.name: Marcinkiewicz
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Henryk, Beata, Elzbieta, Janina.
Polish: patronymic from the personal name Marcinek, a petform of Marcin (see Martin ).

variants.name: Mar
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Gim, Wai, Fong, Hong, Man Chung, Moo, Mun, Quon, Wing, Yat, Bok, Chun.
Spanish, Portuguese, Galician, Asturian-Leonese, and Catalan: topographic name for someone living by the sea, from mar ‘sea’ (from Latin mare).
German: nickname from Middle High German mar(w) ‘tender, delicate’, or an altered form of Mahr, a topographic name from mar ‘lake, swamp, stagnant water’.

en.lifeSummary: When Sarah Elizabeth Martin was born on 7 April 1385, in England, her father, Martin, was 31 and her mother, Martin, was 26. She had at least 2 daughters with Theodore Benson Hardinger. She died on 17 February 1440, in her hometown, at the age of 54.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sarah Elizabeth Martin est née le 7 avril 1385, à Angleterre, son père, Martin, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Martin, avait 26 ans. Elle a eu au moins 2 filles avec Theodore Benson Hardinger. Elle est décédée le 17 février 1440, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 54 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sarah Elizabeth Martin am 7. April 1385 in England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Martin 31 und ihre Mutter Martin 26. Sie hatte mit Theodore Benson Hardinger mindestens 2 Töchter. Sie starb am 17. Februar 1440 im Alter von 54 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sarah Elizabeth Martin è nata il il 7 aprile 1385 nel England, sua padre, Martin, aveva 31 anni e sua madre, Martin, aveva 26 anni. Lei avuto almeno 2 figlie con Theodore Benson Hardinger. Morì il 17 febbraio 1440, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 54 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Sarah Elizabeth Martin(이)가 1385년 4월 7일 영국에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Martin(은)는 31세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Martin(은)는 26세였다. 그녀는 Theodore Benson Hardinger(와)과 적어도 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1440년 2월 17일 그녀의 고향에서 54세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sarah Elizabeth Martin родилась 7 апреля 1385 г. в местности Англия, её отцу по имени Martin было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Martin, 26. У неё было по крайней мере 2 дочери от Theodore Benson Hardinger. Она умерла 17 февраля 1440 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 54 лет.
